2603.09943.title: PathMem: Toward Cognition-Aligned Memory Transformation for Pathology MLLMs
2603.09943.paper_summary: Computational pathology demands both visual pattern recognition and dynamic integration of structured domain knowledge, including taxonomy, grading criteria, and clinical evidence. In practice, diagnostic reasoning requires linking morphological evidence with formal diagnostic and grading criteria. Although multimodal large language models (MLLMs) demonstrate strong vision language reasoning capabilities, they lack explicit mechanisms for structured knowledge integration and interpretable memory control. As a result, existing models struggle to consistently incorporate pathology-specific diagnostic standards during reasoning. Inspired by the hierarchical memory process of human pathologists, we propose PathMem, a memory-centric multimodal framework for pathology MLLMs. PathMem organizes structured pathology knowledge as a long-term memory (LTM) and introduces a Memory Transformer that models the dynamic transition from LTM to working memory (WM) through multimodal memory activation and context-aware knowledge grounding, enabling context-aware memory refinement for downstream reasoning. PathMem achieves SOTA performance across benchmarks, improving WSI-Bench report generation (12.8% WSI-Precision, 10.1% WSI-Relevance) and open-ended diagnosis by 9.7% and 8.9% over prior WSI-based models.
2603.09943.paper_summary_zh: 計算病理學要求同時具備視覺模式識別和結構化領域知識的動態整合，包括分類法、分級標準和臨床證據。<lb/>在實踐中，診斷推理需要將形態證據與正式的診斷和分級標準聯繫起來。<lb/>儘管多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）展現出強大的視覺語言推理能力，但它們缺乏明確的結構化知識整合機制和可解釋的記憶控制。<lb/>因此，現有模型在推理過程中難以持續納入病理特定的診斷標準。<lb/>受到人類病理學家層次記憶過程的啟發，我們提出了PathMem，一個以記憶為中心的多模態框架，用於病理MLLMs。<lb/>PathMem將結構化的病理知識組織為長期記憶（LTM），並引入一個記憶Transformer，通過多模態記憶激活和上下文感知的知識基礎，建模LTM到工作記憶（WM）的動態轉換，使得上下文感知的記憶細化能夠支持下游推理。<lb/>PathMem在各基準測試中達到了SOTA性能，改善了WSI-Bench報告生成（WSI-精確度提高12.8%，WSI-相關性提高10.1%）以及開放式診斷，分別比之前的WSI模型提高了9.7%和8.9%。
2603.09943.author: Jinyue Li et.al.
2603.09943.authors: Jinyue Li, Yuci Liang, Qiankun Li, Xinheng Lyu, Jiayu Qian, Huabao Chen, Kun Wang, Zhigang Zeng, Anil Anthony Bharath, Yang Liu
